channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A private European aerospace startup completes the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle
item.description: A rocket by a private European aerospace company launched from Norway on Sunday and crashed into the sea 30 seconds later.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After Pure Storage, CIA-backed VC invests in ceramic-based startup that wants to build Exabyte-class storage
item.description: CIA-backed In-Q-Tel invests in ceramic-based storage startup Cerabyte to advance ultra-durable archival solutions for government files.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Tesla protests across Bay Area ignite Trump resistance, but intra-party strife ahead
item.description: After almost stunned paralysis among Democrats in the early weeks of Trump’s onslaught of executive orders, the Trump resistance is gaining traction.
item.dc:creator: Julia Prodis Sulek, The Mercury News

item.title: New wave of smaller, cheaper nuclear reactors sends US states racing to attract the industry
item.description: U.S. states are positioning themselves to compete for newer, cheaper nuclear reactors being developed as communities and tech giants compete in a race for electricity.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: California is spending $100 million to track methane emissions with satellites
item.description: The Golden State will soon start using satellite technology to track down greenhouse gas emissions that may otherwise go undetected from sites around such as oil and gas operations, landfills and livestock facilities.
item.dc:creator: Rob Nikolewski, The Mercury News

item.title: La transformación digital, esencial para la supervivencia del sector asegurador
item.description: Los beneficios de la transformación digital ya son palpables para el sector asegurador. Así lo constata el informe Perspectivas de tecnología en seguros 2025: transformando la innovación en ventaja competitiva que firma Concentrix.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI culture war: Hidden bias in training models may push political propaganda
item.description: The release of Chinese-made DeepSeek has generated heated debate, but critics have largely ignored a huge problem: the potential for China to use such an AI model to push a cultural and political agenda on the rest of the world.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The RACI matrix: Your blueprint for project success
item.description: Having managed and rescued dozens of projects, and helped others do so, I’ve noted that there is always one critical success factor (CSF) that has either been effectively addressed or missed/messed up: clarity around the roles and responsibilities for each project participant and key stakeholder.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: G2 Names INE 2025 Cybersecurity Training Leader
item.description: INE, a global leader in networking and cybersecurity training and certifications, is proud to announce it is the recipient of twelve badges in G2’s Spring 2025 Report, including Grid Leader for Cybersecurity Professional Development, Online Course Providers, and Technical Skills Development, which h
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The slim phone era is (finally) back
item.description: Smartphones have been around long enough that, to the casual observer, their designs seem to have hit a plateau. And on a functional level, that’s more or less true—we’re all essentially holding the same six-inch-ish rectangle, aside from the occasional foldable exception.
item.dc:creator: Sam Byford, Fast Company

item.title: New Australia CIO appointments
item.description: Congratulations to these ‘movers and shakers’ recently hired or promoted into a new chief information officer, senior IT, or board role in Australia. February and March 2025 appointments John Granger joins Healthscope Healthscope has appointed John Granger as Chief Information Officer.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Researchers claim their protocol can create truly random numbers on a current quantum computer
item.description: A team that included researchers at a US bank says it has created a protocol that can generate certified truly random numbers, opening the possibility that current generation quantum computers can be used for secure applications in finance, cryptography, cybersecurity, and privacy.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fauna to shut down FaunaDB service in May
item.description: Fauna, the provider of the NoSQL database FaunaDB, has said that it will shut down the service by the end of May due to the unavailability of capital required to support the database service and market it.
item.dc:creator: Info World
